reasoning: The 360-day average of 14.500 ACLED fatalities per 30-day period largely reflects a period when Trinidad and Tobago was under an extended State of Emergency (SoE) that successfully suppressed gang violence by up to 60%. This SoE expired on January 31, 2026, and a replacement ZOSO bill to maintain enhanced police powers was rejected by the Senate on January 28, 2026. Without these extra powers, violence is already showing signs of rebounding in February 2026, with news reports confirming double murders and gang-related attacks (e.g., Feb 22, 2026). Furthermore, the Prime Minister promised a 'brutal' post-SoE response, which increases the likelihood of fatal police clashes—events specifically tracked by ACLED. Because the baseline of 14.5 is artificially low due to the strict SoE, and the forecast window (Feb 6 – Mar 8, 2026) perfectly aligns with the immediate post-SoE rebound period, the probability of exceeding the 14.5 average is high.
